objective: Implement atomic hard-cap reserve/claim and fixed-charge settle/complete with full frozen receipts inside the single canonical job ledger.
executable_blocker: The canonical budget profile is exact and receipt-ready, but the ledger still records no reservation or settlement, performs no scope capacity check, and accepts unversioned schema lookalikes.

required: forward every frozen AdmissionGrant budget binding explicitly through BridgeKernel to JobLedger.claim
construct and seal one full BudgetReservation inside the same claim BEGIN IMMEDIATE transaction
validate the entire persisted budget projection and serialize hard-cap plus global idempotency before append
construct retained owned-offline accounting attestation and one full SettlementReceipt inside the same complete transaction
bind settlement into ExecutionReceipt integrity parent refs while keeping receipt-last ordering
use explicit database user_version and exact schema fingerprint before any repair or WAL mutation
retain one table existing event types append-only chain pause fencing nonce uniqueness and reference behavior
forbidden: admission L0 contract authority control CAS ingestion validation config workflow lockfile dependency or unlisted test edit
second table ledger new event type column table rebuild or nonempty legacy migration
untrusted usage metering variable release auto-expiry release retry recovery or multi-attempt
silent schema repair backfill compatibility default skip fallback network live authority or UI

acceptance_commands: python3 -m unittest tests.test_stage1_admission tests.test_stage1_assurance tests.test_stage1_authority_policy tests.test_stage1_control_assurance tests.test_stage1_execution tests.test_stage1_execution_assurance tests.test_stage1_ledger tests.test_stage1_pause_epoch_fencing -v
exact 11-file write-set schema object fingerprint contract dependency private-locator network live-authority and weakening scan
rollback: Before any version-1 ledger exists, Agent 0 discards the worker commit and branch. After version-1 creation, restore a verified pre-version backup or retain version-1-capable code; old unversioned code is forbidden. Synthetic databases may be discarded.
